# truenas_api_key (Resource)

Manages a TrueNAS API key.

~> **Important:** The `key` attribute is only returned when the API key is first created. On subsequent reads, the value is preserved from Terraform state. After `terraform import`, `key` will be null.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "truenas_api_key" "example" {
  name = "terraform-managed-key"
}

output "api_key_id" {
  value = truenas_api_key.example.id
}

output "api_key_value" {
  value     = truenas_api_key.example.key
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the API key.

### Optional

- `expires_at` (String) The expiration date of the API key (ISO 8601 format). If not set, the key does not expire.
- `username` (String) The username associated with the API key. Defaults to the authenticated user.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The creation timestamp of the API key.
- `id` (Number) The unique identifier of the API key.
- `key` (String, Sensitive) The API key value. Only available after creation. Cannot be retrieved after initial creation.
- `revoked` (Boolean) Whether the API key has been revoked.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import truenas_api_key.example 42
```
